concept.title: Who Should Use This Tool
concept.shortdesc: Use this guide if you migrate DITA packages at scale or if you extend migration behavior through plugins.

section.title: Audience 1: Content migration engineers
section.p: You are a migration engineer if you run jobs, inspect artifacts, approve plans, and move content to target packages.
Use this manual to learn how to:

ul.li: Run safe dry-run and apply workflows.
Diagnose failures by action_id, handler, and error type.
Refactor rough package structure with repeatable procedures.

section.title: Audience 2: Plugin developers
section.p: You are a plugin developer if you add new detection logic, planning actions, or execution handlers for migration edge cases.
Use this manual to learn how to:

ul.li: Identify when core behavior is not enough for a corpus.
Validate plugins with plugin validate and integration tests.
Install plugins through the dita_package_processor.plugins entry-point group.

section.title: Skills and prerequisites
section.p: Use this manual effectively by reading DITA maps and topics, running command-line workflows, and inspecting JSON artifacts. For plugin work, you also need Python packaging and automated test experience.
